BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick estimate of the shop's break-even ahead of the second-bay expansion review with Brightpath.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate. No rent; the shop building is family-owned. InstructorPay_Raj line carries the payroll for Nadia and Sam.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Add second bay and a third mechanic
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Added bay clears break-even at about 6,300 dollars of added monthly labor, well under the 12,000 projected.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Strongly positive; expansion pays for itself.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: With the 45,000 dollar SBA loan at a 10 year term
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Debt service of about 520 dollars per month is comfortably covered.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: The expansion is viable; even a conservative half of the projected added labor clears break-even and the SBA debt service.
ActionItems: Finalize SBA financials for Brightpath by Oct 15
Lock Firdale Parts bulk pricing for higher volume
Begin third-mechanic hiring once the loan is approved
